name: Bouchees Pauillacaise (Potato Balls with Meat Filling)
recipeIngredient: 2 lb potatoes, peeled, cut into 1-inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Steam the potatoes until soft.
2. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C).
3. Prepare the meat filling: Melt butter or vegetable oil in a pan. Add shallots and onion, sauté over low heat. Stir in flour and let brown slightly. Mix with beef stock, season with salt and pepper, and reduce by half. Stir in diced meat. Set aside.
4. Process potatoes through a ricer or food mill into a bowl. Add butter and milk. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
5. Beat the mixture well over low heat until very dry and thick (about 3-5 minutes). Gradually add beaten eggs, stirring continuously. The mixture should be thick enough to form balls.
6. Form small balls of the potato mixture, about the size of an egg. Flatten them lightly and make a slight indentation in the center.
7. Heat oil to 365°F (185°C) in a deep fryer or heavy pot. Dip each ball first in beaten egg, then coat with breadcrumbs.
8. Fry the balls in batches until golden brown, about 2 minutes per batch. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
9. Place potato balls in an ovenproof dish. Arrange diced meat filling as a dome over each ball. Bake for 15 minutes.
10. Serve hot.
Note: Leftover roasted or braised beef, chicken, veal, or lamb may be used.
